title: Remove API access and webhooks functionality
description:
Remove or deprecate API access and webhooks features from the platform:

SCOPE ASSESSMENT:
- Identify all existing API endpoints for external access
- Identify all webhook implementations
- Review current usage (if any)
- Assess impact on existing integrations
- Determine deprecation timeline

API ACCESS REMOVAL:
- Remove API key generation functionality
- Remove API key authentication middleware
- Remove API key management UI
- Delete API key database tables
- Remove API documentation pages
- Remove API-specific routes and endpoints
- Clean up API-related environment variables

WEBHOOK REMOVAL:
- Remove webhook subscription endpoints
- Remove webhook delivery service
- Remove webhook signature generation
- Delete webhook subscriptions from database
- Remove webhook configuration UI
- Remove webhook event logging
- Stop webhook retry jobs/workers

DATABASE CLEANUP:
- Drop api_keys table (if exists)
- Drop webhook_subscriptions table (if exists)
- Drop webhook_events table (if exists)
- Drop webhook_delivery_log table (if exists)
- Remove API-related columns from other tables
- Clean up orphaned records

AFFECTED INTEGRATIONS:
- Review Zapier integration dependencies
  * May need to keep minimal webhook support for Zapier
  * Or migrate to different integration approach
- Check QuickBooks integration
- Check Google Calendar integration
- Check Stripe webhook handling (keep - required for Stripe)
- Identify any custom integrations using API

CODE REMOVAL:
Files/modules to remove or update:
- /lib/api/ directory (if exists)
- /lib/webhooks/ directory (if exists)
- /app/routes/api/ - external API routes
- /app/routes/settings/api-keys.tsx (if exists)
- /app/routes/settings/webhooks.tsx (if exists)
- API authentication middleware
- Webhook delivery service
- API rate limiting logic
- Webhook signature verification

SETTINGS/UI CLEANUP:
- Remove API Keys page from settings menu
- Remove Webhooks page from settings menu
- Remove API documentation links
- Update settings navigation
- Remove API-related form components
- Remove webhook configuration forms

JOBS/WORKERS:
- Remove webhook delivery worker
- Remove webhook retry job
- Remove API usage tracking job
- Clean up job queue configurations
- Update worker processes

SECURITY CLEANUP:
- Remove API key hashing utilities
- Remove webhook signature generation
- Remove API-scoped permissions
- Update security documentation
- Remove API-related audit logs (or archive)

ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES:
Remove or deprecate:
- API_ENABLED
- API_RATE_LIMIT_*
- WEBHOOK_SIGNING_SECRET
- API_VERSION
- Update .env.example
- Update deployment configurations

MIGRATION PATH (if keeping some functionality):
- If keeping Zapier webhooks:
  * Separate Zapier-specific webhook implementation
  * Use internal webhook system only
  * Document Zapier as special case
  
- If keeping Stripe webhooks:
  * Keep /api/webhooks/stripe endpoint
  * Remove generic webhook framework
  * Stripe webhooks are internal, not user-configurable

DOCUMENTATION UPDATES:
- Remove API documentation pages
- Remove webhook setup guides
- Update integration documentation
- Add deprecation notice (if phased removal)
- Update changelog
- Update README

TESTING:
- Remove API endpoint tests
- Remove webhook delivery tests
- Remove API authentication tests
- Update integration tests
- Verify existing functionality still works
- Test that removed endpoints return 404

COMMUNICATION (if users affected):
- Deprecation announcement
- Migration timeline
- Alternative solutions
- Support for affected users
- Email notifications to API key holders

ALTERNATIVES TO CONSIDER:
Instead of complete removal, consider:
- Keep internal webhooks only (for system integrations)
- Keep read-only API for specific use cases
- Implement OAuth for future integrations
- Use Zapier as the official integration method

PHASED REMOVAL (if needed):
Phase 1 (Week 1-2):
- Mark features as deprecated
- Add deprecation warnings to UI
- Communicate to users
- Provide migration guides

Phase 2 (Week 3-4):
- Disable new API key creation
- Disable new webhook subscriptions
- Existing keys/webhooks still work

Phase 3 (Week 5-6):
- Remove UI components
- Archive existing API keys
- Stop webhook deliveries

Phase 4 (Week 7-8):
- Remove backend code
- Drop database tables
- Final cleanup

ROLLBACK PLAN:
- Database backup before table drops
- Code branch before removal
- Feature flag to re-enable if needed
- Timeline for decision to rollback

EXCEPTIONS TO KEEP:
✓ Stripe webhooks - Required for payment processing
  * Keep /api/webhooks/stripe
  * Keep Stripe webhook verification
  
✓ Internal system webhooks (if used)
  * Job completion notifications
  * System event broadcasts
  
? Zapier webhooks - Decide based on Zapier integration plans
  * If Zapier integration stays, keep minimal webhook support
  * Or migrate to Zapier Platform REST Hooks

FILES TO REVIEW:
- lib/integrations/zapier.server.ts
- app/routes/api/webhooks/
- Database schema files
- Authentication middleware
- Integration documentation

title: Fully implement Zapier integration for workflow automation
description:
Complete Zapier integration to enable no-code workflow automation and third-party app connections:

ZAPIER APP PLATFORM:
- Build official DiveStreams Zapier app
- Submit to Zapier App Directory
- Public vs Private app versions
- App branding and description
- Category selection (Business Management, CRM)
- Support and documentation links

AUTHENTICATION:
- Implement API Key authentication for Zapier
- OAuth2 authentication option (future enhancement)
- Secure key generation and management
- Key rotation/revocation
- Per-organization API keys
- Scoped permissions (read vs write)
- Test authentication endpoint

TRIGGERS (Send data FROM DiveStreams TO other apps):
- New Booking Created
  * Trigger when customer books a trip
  * Sample data: customer name, email, trip details, booking number
  * Polling URL: GET /api/zapier/triggers/bookings/new
  
- Booking Status Changed
  * Trigger on confirmed/cancelled/completed status
  * Include previous and new status
  * Polling URL: GET /api/zapier/triggers/bookings/status-changed
  
- New Customer Created
  * Trigger when new customer registers
  * Customer profile data
  * Polling URL: GET /api/zapier/triggers/customers/new
  
- Payment Received
  * Trigger on successful payment
  * Payment amount, method, booking reference
  * Polling URL: GET /api/zapier/triggers/payments/received
  
- Trip Fully Booked
  * Trigger when trip reaches capacity
  * Trip details and waitlist info
  * Polling URL: GET /api/zapier/triggers/trips/fully-booked
  
- Review Submitted
  * Trigger when customer submits review
  * Review rating, text, customer info
  * Polling URL: GET /api/zapier/triggers/reviews/new
  
- Equipment Rental
  * Trigger on equipment rental
  * Equipment details, rental period
  * Polling URL: GET /api/zapier/triggers/equipment/rented

ACTIONS (Send data FROM other apps TO DiveStreams):
- Create Booking
  * Create new booking via Zapier
  * Input: customer details, trip ID, participants
  * Endpoint: POST /api/zapier/actions/bookings/create
  
- Update Booking Status
  * Change booking status (confirm, cancel)
  * Input: booking ID, new status, reason
  * Endpoint: POST /api/zapier/actions/bookings/update-status
  
- Create Customer
  * Add new customer to system
  * Input: name, email, phone, emergency contact
  * Endpoint: POST /api/zapier/actions/customers/create
  
- Add Customer Note
  * Append note to customer profile
  * Input: customer ID, note text, category
  * Endpoint: POST /api/zapier/actions/customers/add-note
  
- Send Email via Template
  * Trigger email to customer using template
  * Input: customer email, template ID, variables
  * Endpoint: POST /api/zapier/actions/emails/send
  
- Add to Waitlist
  * Add customer to trip waitlist
  * Input: customer ID, trip ID
  * Endpoint: POST /api/zapier/actions/waitlist/add

SEARCHES (Find existing data):
- Find Booking
  * Search by booking number, customer email, or trip
  * Endpoint: GET /api/zapier/searches/bookings
  
- Find Customer
  * Search by email, phone, or name
  * Endpoint: GET /api/zapier/searches/customers
  
- Find Trip
  * Search by name, date, or location
  * Endpoint: GET /api/zapier/searches/trips

POLLING & WEBHOOKS:
- REST Hooks (recommended by Zapier)
- Implement subscribe/unsubscribe endpoints:
  * POST /api/zapier/hooks/subscribe
  * DELETE /api/zapier/hooks/unsubscribe/:id
- Store webhook subscriptions in database
- Trigger webhooks on relevant events
- Dedupe key for preventing duplicates
- Webhook retry logic (3 attempts, exponential backoff)

API ENDPOINTS:
- GET /api/zapier/auth/test - Test authentication
- GET /api/zapier/triggers/:trigger_name - Polling triggers
- POST /api/zapier/actions/:action_name - Execute actions
- GET /api/zapier/searches/:search_name - Search queries
- POST /api/zapier/hooks/subscribe - Subscribe to webhook
- DELETE /api/zapier/hooks/unsubscribe/:id - Unsubscribe
- GET /api/zapier/schema - Dynamic field schema

DATABASE SCHEMA:
- zapier_api_keys table:
  * organization_id
  * api_key (hashed)
  * key_name
  * scopes (JSON array)
  * last_used_at
  * expires_at
  * created_by_user_id
  * created_at
  * revoked_at

- zapier_webhook_subscriptions table:
  * subscription_id
  * organization_id
  * trigger_name (booking.created, payment.received, etc)
  * target_url (Zapier webhook URL)
  * created_at
  * last_triggered_at
  * is_active

- zapier_event_log table:
  * event_id
  * organization_id
  * trigger_name
  * webhook_url
  * payload (JSON)
  * status (success/failed)
  * status_code
  * error_message
  * triggered_at
  * retry_count

ZAPIER APP CONFIGURATION:
- app.yaml / zapier.json definition
- Trigger definitions with input/output schemas
- Action definitions with input/output schemas
- Search definitions
- Dynamic dropdowns (trips, customers)
- Field help text and placeholders
- Sample data for testing
- Error handling messages

DYNAMIC DROPDOWNS:
- List Trips (for booking actions)
  * Endpoint: GET /api/zapier/dropdowns/trips
  * Return: [{id, name, date}]
  
- List Customers (for note actions)
  * Endpoint: GET /api/zapier/dropdowns/customers
  * Return: [{id, name, email}]
  
- List Email Templates
  * Endpoint: GET /api/zapier/dropdowns/email-templates
  * Return: [{id, name, subject}]

SAMPLE DATA:
- Provide realistic sample data for each trigger/action
- Used by Zapier for Zap setup preview
- Should match actual API response format
- Include all possible fields

UI COMPONENTS:
- Zapier integration page in settings
- API key generation interface
- Active Zap viewer (if using Zapier Platform UI)
- Connected apps dashboard
- Event log viewer
- API usage statistics
- Key management (create, revoke, rotate)
- Documentation links

POPULAR ZAP TEMPLATES:
- "New Booking → Add to Google Sheets"
- "New Booking → Send Slack notification"
- "New Customer → Add to Mailchimp"
- "Payment Received → Update QuickBooks"
- "Booking Cancelled → Send cancellation email via Gmail"
- "Trip Fully Booked → Create Google Calendar event"
- "New Review → Post to company Slack channel"

ERROR HANDLING:
- Invalid API key (401 Unauthorized)
- Missing required fields (400 Bad Request)
- Resource not found (404 Not Found)
- Rate limiting (429 Too Many Requests)
- Server errors (500)
- Helpful error messages for Zapier users
- Retry-After headers for rate limits

SECURITY:
- API key hashing in database
- Rate limiting per organization
- Input validation and sanitization
- SQL injection prevention
- CSRF not needed (API-only)
- Webhook signature verification (optional)
- Scope-based permissions
- Audit logging

RATE LIMITING:
- 100 requests per minute per API key
- 5000 requests per day per organization
- Return 429 with Retry-After header
- Communicate limits in Zapier app docs

TESTING:
- Unit tests for all triggers/actions/searches
- Integration tests with Zapier CLI
- Test webhook subscriptions
- Test dynamic dropdowns
- Test deduplication
- Test error scenarios
- Load testing for popular triggers

DOCUMENTATION:
- Zapier app setup guide
- Authentication instructions
- Available triggers/actions/searches
- Field descriptions and examples
- Common Zap templates
- Troubleshooting guide
- API rate limits
- Changelog for app updates
- Environment variables:
  * ZAPIER_API_ENABLED (true/false)
  * ZAPIER_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE
  * ZAPIER_RATE_LIMIT_PER_DAY

MONITORING:
- Track Zapier API usage per organization
- Monitor webhook delivery success rate
- Alert on high failure rates
- Track most popular triggers/actions
- Performance metrics (response times)

ZAPIER PLATFORM TOOLS:
- Use Zapier Platform CLI for development
- zapier test - Run tests
- zapier validate - Validate app definition
- zapier push - Deploy to Zapier
- zapier promote - Promote to production
- zapier logs - View production logs

SUBMISSION & REVIEW:
- Zapier app submission checklist
- App review process preparation
- Beta testing phase
- Public launch preparation
- Zapier brand guidelines compliance

title: Fix E2E test authentication and timeout failures
description: Fix 2 failing E2E tests: public-site.spec.ts (beforeAll timeout) and training-module.spec.ts (authentication failure). Root cause: dynamic Date.now() emails couldn't be shared between tests + global timeout override issue.

comments.author: tom-gibson-med
comments.text:
Completed E2E test fixes:
1. Changed all test files to use fixed shared email 'e2e-user@example.com' instead of dynamic Date.now() emails
2. Added test.setTimeout(60000) in public-site.spec.ts beforeAll hook to override global 30s timeout
3. Files modified: full-workflow.spec.ts, public-site.spec.ts, training-module.spec.ts
4. Committed in 14ff188 and pushed to staging
5. CI/CD pipeline running with fixes

title: Add staging deployment override for E2E failures and fix flaky tests
description:
1. Add CI/CD override to allow staging deployment despite E2E failures
2. Fix flaky E2E tests with authentication state issues
3. Deploy to staging and verify

comments.author: tom-gibson-med
comments.text:
✅ Successfully deployed to staging with E2E override enabled
- Added continue-on-error to E2E job in deploy.yml
- Fixed 4 flaky test assertions with proper auth checks
- Deployment completed at 2026-01-18 19:01:06 UTC
- Pipeline: https://github.com/shooter51/divestreams-v2/actions/runs/21116537768

title: Add comprehensive training seed data for dive certifications
description:
Add comprehensive training seed data for dive certifications including agencies, levels, courses, and prerequisites:

TRAINING AGENCIES:
- PADI (Professional Association of Diving Instructors)
- SSI (Scuba Schools International)  
- NAUI (National Association of Underwater Instructors)
- SDI/TDI (Scuba Diving International / Technical Diving International)
- CMAS (World Underwater Federation)
- BSAC (British Sub-Aqua Club)
- GUE (Global Underwater Explorers)

CERTIFICATION LEVELS:
Beginner: Discover Scuba, Scuba Diver, Open Water Diver
Intermediate: Advanced Open Water, Rescue Diver
Professional: Divemaster, Assistant Instructor, Instructor
Specialties: Deep, Wreck, Night, Nitrox, Navigation, etc.
Technical: Tec 40/45/50, Trimix

SAMPLE COURSES:
- Complete course records with pricing, duration, prerequisites
- Course schedules (weekend/weekday options)
- Equipment requirements
- Training materials needed

DATABASE SEEDS:
- seeds/training-agencies.sql
- seeds/certification-levels.sql
- seeds/courses.sql
- seeds/course-prerequisites.sql

Include realistic data with proper prerequisite chains, age requirements, depth limits, and certification numbers.

title: Fix public-site E2E test setup
description:
Public site E2E tests require publicSiteSettings.enabled = true but test setup doesn't configure this.

Root cause: The tenant public site layout checks settings.enabled and redirects to /?site=disabled if false.

Fix options:
1. Add database seed/fixture to enable public site for e2etest tenant
2. Add a setup step in public-site.spec.ts that enables public site via admin settings
3. Modify the E2E database schema setup in CI to include public site enabled

Tests affected:
- tests/e2e/workflow/public-site.spec.ts (44 tests currently skipped)

title: Fix all URLs to respect base URL environment (staging vs production)
description: Audit and fix all hardcoded URLs throughout the application to use environment-aware base URLs. DNS instructions, links, and all references should work correctly in staging and production.
close_reason: Fixed all hardcoded URLs to respect APP_URL environment variable. Updated 11 files across admin panel, tenant settings, email triggers, auth, integrations, and middleware. Fixed unit tests to expect environment-aware URLs. Deployed to staging via CI/CD pipeline #21117463232.

title: Fix flaky public-site.spec.ts A.3 contact page test - beforeAll hook silently fails to enable public site, causing redirect to /?site=disabled
Configure APP_URL environment variable on staging VPS
description: The staging VPS .env file is missing APP_URL=https://staging.divestreams.com, causing all URLs in the app to default to production domains. Need to SSH to VPS and update .env file.
close_reason: Added APP_URL=https://staging.divestreams.com to /docker/divestreams-staging/.env and restarted app container. DNS setup instructions and all URLs should now show staging.divestreams.com domains.

title: Fully implement Stripe integration for payments and subscriptions
description:
Complete Stripe integration for the platform including:

PAYMENT PROCESSING:
- Customer creation and management in Stripe
- Payment method storage and validation
- One-time payment processing for bookings
- Refund handling
- Payment status webhooks

SUBSCRIPTION MANAGEMENT:
- Premium plan subscription setup
- Subscription lifecycle (create, update, cancel)
- Plan upgrades/downgrades
- Proration handling
- Trial periods
- Subscription status webhooks

WEBHOOKS:
- Implement webhook endpoint at /api/webhooks/stripe
- Handle all critical webhook events:
  * payment_intent.succeeded
  * payment_intent.failed
  * customer.subscription.created
  * customer.subscription.updated
  * customer.subscription.deleted
  * invoice.payment_succeeded
  * invoice.payment_failed
  * checkout.session.completed
- Webhook signature verification
- Idempotency handling

SECURITY:
- Secure API key storage (already using env vars)
- Webhook signature verification
- PCI compliance considerations
- Error handling and logging

UI COMPONENTS:
- Payment method selection form
- Card input (using Stripe Elements)
- Subscription management page
- Payment history
- Invoice downloads

TESTING:
- Unit tests for Stripe service functions
- Integration tests with Stripe test mode
- Webhook event simulation tests
- Error scenario testing

DOCUMENTATION:
- Setup instructions for Stripe account
- Webhook configuration guide
- Testing procedures
- Environment variable documentation

title: Fully implement QuickBooks integration for accounting and invoicing
description:
Complete QuickBooks Online integration for automated accounting, invoicing, and financial management:

OAUTH & AUTHENTICATION:
- Implement OAuth2 flow for QuickBooks Online API
- Company authorization and consent
- Token storage and refresh handling
- Multi-tenant QuickBooks connections (per organization)
- Company selection UI (multi-company support)
- Disconnect/revoke access functionality
- Sandbox vs Production environment support

CUSTOMER SYNC:
- Sync dive shop customers to QuickBooks as Customers
- Two-way sync (QB → DiveStreams, DiveStreams → QB)
- Customer creation with contact details
- Customer updates (email, phone, address)
- Customer deduplication and matching
- Custom field mapping
- Sync customer groups/categories

INVOICE AUTOMATION:
- Auto-create invoices in QuickBooks for bookings
- Invoice line items (trip fees, equipment rentals, courses)
- Tax calculation and application
- Discount handling
- Deposit invoices vs full invoices
- Invoice status sync (draft, sent, paid, overdue)
- Payment terms configuration
- Custom invoice templates

PAYMENT SYNC:
- Record payments from bookings in QuickBooks
- Payment method mapping (card, cash, check, bank transfer)
- Payment application to invoices
- Partial payment handling
- Refund recording
- Payment reconciliation
- Bank deposit integration

PRODUCT/SERVICE SYNC:
- Sync dive trips as Services in QuickBooks
- Equipment rentals as Products
- Courses as Services
- Price tier management
- Tax codes and rates
- Income account mapping
- COGS tracking for equipment

EXPENSE TRACKING:
- Record trip expenses (boat fuel, guide wages, equipment maintenance)
- Vendor management
- Expense categorization
- Receipt attachments
- Billable vs non-billable expenses
- Expense approval workflow

SALES RECEIPTS:
- Create sales receipts for paid-at-booking transactions
- Immediate payment recording
- POS integration for walk-in bookings
- Receipt email automation

FINANCIAL REPORTING:
- Revenue by trip type/location
- Customer payment history
- Outstanding invoices report
- Sales tax summary
- Profit & loss by service category
- Cash flow projections

WEBHOOK & REAL-TIME SYNC:
- Implement QuickBooks webhooks
- Listen for customer/invoice/payment changes
- Real-time sync triggers
- Webhook verification and security
- Event processing queue
- Retry mechanism for failed syncs

SYNC SETTINGS & PREFERENCES:
- Enable/disable QuickBooks sync per organization
- Choose what to sync (customers, invoices, payments, products)
- Sync direction configuration
- Account mapping (income, COGS, AR, sales tax)
- Default payment terms
- Invoice numbering format
- Tax settings per location
- Sync frequency (real-time, hourly, daily)

API ENDPOINTS:
- POST /api/integrations/quickbooks/connect - Start OAuth flow
- POST /api/integrations/quickbooks/disconnect - Revoke access
- GET /api/integrations/quickbooks/status - Check connection status
- POST /api/integrations/quickbooks/sync - Manual sync trigger
- POST /api/integrations/quickbooks/sync/customers - Sync customers only
- POST /api/integrations/quickbooks/sync/invoices - Sync invoices only
- POST /api/webhooks/quickbooks - Webhook receiver
- GET /api/integrations/quickbooks/accounts - List QB chart of accounts
- GET /api/integrations/quickbooks/settings - Get sync settings
- PUT /api/integrations/quickbooks/settings - Update sync settings

DATABASE SCHEMA:
- quickbooks_connections table:
  * organization_id
  * realm_id (QuickBooks company ID)
  * access_token (encrypted)
  * refresh_token (encrypted)
  * token_expires_at
  * sync_enabled
  * sync_settings (JSON)
  * environment (sandbox/production)
  * connected_at
  * last_sync_at
  * created_at, updated_at

- quickbooks_sync_mappings table:
  * organization_id
  * entity_type (customer/product/service/invoice)
  * local_id
  * quickbooks_id
  * last_synced_at
  * sync_status

- quickbooks_sync_log table:
  * sync_id
  * organization_id
  * sync_type (customer/invoice/payment/product)
  * entity_id
  * quickbooks_entity_id
  * direction (to_qb/from_qb)
  * status (success/failed/pending)
  * error_message
  * request_payload
  * response_payload
  * synced_at

- quickbooks_account_mappings table:
  * organization_id
  * mapping_type (income/cogs/sales_tax/ar)
  * account_id (QuickBooks account ID)
  * account_name

UI COMPONENTS:
- QuickBooks connection page in settings
- OAuth consent flow UI
- Company selection (for multi-company accounts)
- Sync settings configuration form
- Account mapping interface
- Sync status dashboard
- Manual sync buttons
- Sync history/logs viewer
- Error resolution UI
- Financial reports viewer
- Invoice preview before sync

ACCOUNTING FEATURES:
- Chart of accounts mapping
- Class/department tracking
- Location tracking (for multi-location dive shops)
- Sales tax handling by location
- Multi-currency support
- Fiscal year configuration
- Journal entry creation

ERROR HANDLING:
- Token expiration and refresh
- API rate limiting (500 requests/min, 10k/day)
- Network failures and retries
- Duplicate detection
- Validation errors
- Permission errors
- Graceful degradation
- Sync conflict resolution
- Orphaned record handling

SECURITY:
- Secure token storage (encryption at rest)
- OAuth state parameter validation
- CSRF protection
- Webhook signature verification
- Scope limitation (minimal permissions needed)
- Audit logging for financial data access
- PII protection compliance

TESTING:
- Unit tests for QuickBooks service functions
- Integration tests with QB Sandbox
- OAuth flow testing
- Webhook event simulation
- Sync scenarios (customer, invoice, payment)
- Error handling tests
- Idempotency tests
- Rate limiting handling

DOCUMENTATION:
- QuickBooks developer account setup
- OAuth app configuration
- Sandbox testing guide
- Production deployment checklist
- Webhook configuration
- Account mapping guide
- Troubleshooting common issues
- API quota management
- Environment variables:
  * QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_ID
  * QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_SECRET
  * QUICKBOOKS_REDIRECT_URI
  * QUICKBOOKS_ENVIRONMENT (sandbox/production)
  * QUICKBOOKS_WEBHOOK_VERIFIER_TOKEN

COMPLIANCE:
- QuickBooks terms of service compliance
- Data retention policies
- Financial data backup
- SOC 2 compliance considerations
- GDPR/privacy law compliance for customer data

title: Partner with dive equipment manufacturers for catalog integration
description:
Establish partnerships with dive equipment manufacturers to integrate their product catalogs into the platform for equipment rental and sales:

TARGET MANUFACTURERS:
Major Brands:
- Aqua Lung / Apeks
- Scubapro
- Mares
- Cressi
- Oceanic
- Suunto
- Garmin (dive computers)
- Shearwater (dive computers)
- Hollis
- Zeagle
- Atomic Aquatics
- Fourth Element (exposure protection)
- DUI (drysuits)
- Bare (exposure protection)
- Aqualung
- Sherwood

PARTNERSHIP OBJECTIVES:
- Access to official product catalogs
- Product images and specifications
- Real-time pricing updates
- Inventory availability
- Product discontinuation notices
- New product launches
- Marketing materials
- Affiliate/dealer programs

CATALOG DATA NEEDED:
Product Information:
- SKU / Part Number
- Product Name
- Category (BCD, Regulator, Wetsuit, Computer, etc.)
- Subcategory
- Detailed description
- Technical specifications
- Size options
- Color options
- Weight
- Dimensions
- Materials
- Certifications (CE, ISO)

Pricing:
- MSRP (Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price)
- Dealer/wholesale pricing
- Volume discounts
- Seasonal pricing
- Currency options
- Tax information

Media:
- High-resolution product images (multiple angles)
- Product videos
- 360-degree views
- Size charts
- User manuals (PDFs)
- Specification sheets
- Marketing materials

INTEGRATION METHODS:
API Integration:
- RESTful API access to catalog
- Real-time inventory sync
- Automated price updates
- Product feed webhooks
- OAuth authentication

File-Based:
- CSV/Excel catalog exports
- Scheduled updates (daily/weekly)
- FTP/SFTP delivery
- Email attachments

Manual:
- Quarterly catalog PDFs
- Dealer portals access
- Price lists

OUTREACH STRATEGY:
Initial Contact:
- Identify manufacturer dealer/partnership contacts
- Prepare partnership proposal deck
- Highlight platform benefits:
  * Increased product visibility
  * Direct sales channel to dive shops
  * Marketing exposure
  * Data insights on popular products
  
Email Template:
Subject: Partnership Opportunity - DiveStreams Equipment Catalog Integration

Body:
Dear [Manufacturer] Partnership Team,

We are reaching out regarding a partnership opportunity for DiveStreams, a comprehensive dive shop management platform serving [X] dive centers worldwide.

We would like to integrate your product catalog to enable our dive shops to:
- Offer your equipment for rental
- Facilitate retail sales
- Provide accurate product information to customers
- Streamline equipment ordering

Benefits for [Manufacturer]:
- Increased brand visibility across our network
- Direct sales channel to dive operators
- Real-time product exposure
- Marketing and promotional opportunities

We are interested in discussing:
- Catalog data access (API or file-based)
- Dealer pricing programs
- Marketing collaboration
- Technical integration support

Would you be available for a brief call to explore this partnership?

Best regards,
[Your Name]
DiveStreams Partnership Team

Follow-up:
- Schedule intro calls
- Present platform demo
- Discuss technical requirements
- Negotiate terms
- Execute partnership agreements

PARTNERSHIP TIERS:
Platinum Partners:
- Full API integration
- Real-time sync
- Featured placement
- Co-marketing opportunities
- Dedicated support

Gold Partners:
- Weekly catalog updates
- Priority listing
- Marketing support
- Standard support

Silver Partners:
- Monthly catalog updates
- Standard listing
- Basic support

LEGAL CONSIDERATIONS:
Agreements Needed:
- Partnership/Dealer Agreement
- Data Use Agreement
- Trademark Usage Rights
- Image Licensing
- Pricing Confidentiality (dealer pricing)
- Territory restrictions
- Minimum order quantities
- Return policies

Liability:
- Product information accuracy
- Price accuracy disclaimers
- Warranty handling
- Customer support responsibilities

TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS:
Database Schema:
- equipment_manufacturers table
- equipment_products table
- equipment_categories table
- equipment_pricing table
- equipment_inventory table
- manufacturer_api_credentials table

API Integration:
- Manufacturer API client implementation
- Sync scheduling (cron jobs)
- Error handling and retry logic
- Data validation
- Deduplication

UI Components:
- Equipment catalog browser
- Product detail pages
- Size/option selectors
- Rental vs purchase toggle
- Availability calendar
- Price calculator

BUSINESS MODEL:
Revenue Options:
- Referral commissions (%)
- Affiliate program
- Direct sales markup
- Rental revenue share
- Drop-shipping arrangements

Pricing Strategy:
- Transparent pricing vs markup
- Rental pricing tiers
- Seasonal adjustments
- Member discounts

PILOT PROGRAM:
Phase 1: Single Manufacturer Pilot
- Choose 1-2 manufacturers for initial integration
- Test technical integration
- Validate business model
- Gather feedback

Phase 2: Expand to Top 5
- Add major brands
- Refine integration process
- Improve UI based on feedback

Phase 3: Full Rollout
- Open to all manufacturers
- Self-service onboarding
- Automated catalog sync

METRICS TO TRACK:
Partnership:
- Number of manufacturers contacted
- Response rate
- Partnership conversion rate
- Active partnerships

Technical:
- Catalog sync success rate
- Data accuracy
- API uptime
- Sync latency

Business:
- Products listed per manufacturer
- Customer product views
- Equipment rental bookings
- Direct sales conversions
- Revenue per manufacturer

COMPETITIVE ANALYSIS:
Research how competitors integrate:
- ScubaBoard marketplace
- LeisurePro
- Dive Right In Scuba
- Amazon dive equipment
- Manufacturer direct sales

TIMELINE:
Month 1-2: Research and Outreach
- Identify target manufacturers
- Prepare materials
- Begin outreach

Month 3-4: Pilot Integration
- Sign first partnership
- Technical integration
- Testing

Month 5-6: Expand
- Add 3-5 more manufacturers
- Refine processes

Month 7+: Ongoing
- Continue adding partners
- Optimize integrations
- Monitor performance

DELIVERABLES:
- Partnership proposal deck
- Outreach email templates
- Partnership agreement templates
- Technical integration documentation
- API client implementations
- Catalog sync system
- Equipment catalog UI
- Performance dashboard

title: Allow CI/CD to build even when E2E tests fail
description: Modified workflow to build Docker image when unit tests pass but E2E tests fail. Also fixed flaky contact page E2E test assertion.

title: Implement Training Module Routes
description:
Training module routes are missing. E2E tests exist but routes were never created.

Routes needed:
- /app/training (dashboard)
- /app/training/courses (list, new, :id, :id/edit)
- /app/training/sessions (list, new, :id)
- /app/training/enrollments (list, :id)
- /app/settings/training/agencies
- /app/settings/training/levels

Server functions needed (lib/db/training.server.ts):
- getCourses, getCourse, createCourse, updateCourse, deleteCourse
- getSessions, getSession, createSession, updateSession
- getEnrollments, getEnrollment, createEnrollment, updateEnrollment
- getAgencies, createAgency, getLevels, createLevel

E2E tests already exist in tests/e2e/workflow/training-module.spec.ts (currently skipped)

title: Fully implement Google Calendar integration for trip and booking sync
description:
Complete Google Calendar integration for syncing dive trips, bookings, and staff schedules:

OAUTH & AUTHENTICATION:
- Implement OAuth2 flow for Google Calendar API
- User consent and authorization
- Token storage and refresh handling
- Multi-tenant calendar connections (per organization)
- Calendar selection UI (primary vs other calendars)
- Disconnect/revoke access functionality

CALENDAR SYNC - TRIPS:
- Sync dive trips to Google Calendar
- Two-way sync (Google → DiveStreams, DiveStreams → Google)
- Event creation with trip details (time, location, capacity, price)
- Event updates when trip details change
- Event deletion when trip is cancelled
- Recurring trip handling
- Color coding by trip type/status

CALENDAR SYNC - BOOKINGS:
- Create calendar events for confirmed bookings
- Include customer details, participant count
- Booking status updates (confirmed, cancelled, waitlist)
- Attendee management
- Reminders and notifications
- Attach booking confirmation details

CALENDAR SYNC - STAFF SCHEDULES:
- Staff availability calendar integration
- Guide assignments to trips
- Shift scheduling
- Conflict detection
- Multi-calendar support for staff members

WEBHOOK & PUSH NOTIFICATIONS:
- Implement Google Calendar push notifications
- Watch for external calendar changes
- Handle event created/updated/deleted from Google
- Sync conflicts resolution
- Notification channel management
- Channel renewal/expiration handling

SYNC SETTINGS & PREFERENCES:
- Enable/disable calendar sync per organization
- Choose what to sync (trips, bookings, staff)
- Sync direction (one-way vs two-way)
- Event visibility settings (public/private)
- Timezone handling
- Custom event templates
- Sync frequency configuration

API ENDPOINTS:
- POST /api/integrations/google-calendar/connect - Start OAuth flow
- POST /api/integrations/google-calendar/disconnect - Revoke access
- GET /api/integrations/google-calendar/status - Check connection status
- POST /api/integrations/google-calendar/sync - Manual sync trigger
- POST /api/webhooks/google-calendar - Webhook receiver for push notifications
- GET /api/integrations/google-calendar/calendars - List available calendars

DATABASE SCHEMA:
- google_calendar_connections table:
  * organization_id
  * user_id
  * access_token (encrypted)
  * refresh_token (encrypted)
  * calendar_id
  * sync_enabled
  * sync_settings (JSON)
  * watch_channel_id
  * watch_expiration
  * created_at, updated_at

- calendar_sync_log table:
  * sync_id
  * organization_id
  * sync_type (trip/booking/staff)
  * entity_id (trip_id/booking_id)
  * google_event_id
  * sync_direction (to_google/from_google)
  * status (success/failed)
  * error_message
  * synced_at

UI COMPONENTS:
- Calendar connection page in settings
- OAuth consent flow UI
- Calendar selection dropdown
- Sync settings configuration form
- Sync status indicators
- Manual sync button
- Sync history/logs viewer
- Conflict resolution UI
- Calendar preview/embed

ERROR HANDLING:
- Token expiration and refresh
- API rate limiting (quota exceeded)
- Network failures and retries
- Sync conflict resolution
- Invalid calendar permissions
- Deleted external events
- Graceful degradation when API is down

SECURITY:
- Secure token storage (encryption at rest)
- OAuth state parameter validation
- CSRF protection
- Webhook signature verification
- Scope limitation (minimal permissions)
- Audit logging for calendar access

TESTING:
- Unit tests for calendar service functions
- Integration tests with Google Calendar API (test mode)
- OAuth flow testing
- Webhook event simulation
- Sync conflict scenarios
- Rate limiting handling
- Token refresh testing

DOCUMENTATION:
- Google Cloud Console setup guide
- OAuth credentials configuration
- Webhook setup instructions
- Sync behavior documentation
- Troubleshooting guide
- API quota management
- Environment variables needed:
  * GOOGLE_CALENDAR_CLIENT_ID
  * GOOGLE_CALENDAR_CLIENT_SECRET
  * GOOGLE_CALENDAR_REDIRECT_URI
